source: Invest Like the Best — AI, semiconductors, and the robotic frontier (EP.385)
summary: Tesla FSD is on the same scaling law trajectory as LLMs and is approaching a step-function improvement that will be 'abjectly humiliating' to skeptics within 6-18 months
summary_extended: Baker argues that Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) is following the same scaling law trajectory as large language models, and that the transition from FSD 12.3 (fully deep learning, no human-written code) to FSD 12.5 (running on AI4 hardware) represents roughly GPT-2 to GPT-2.5 level capability — with a rapid jump to GPT-4 equivalent capability expected within 6-18 months as Tesla scales its compute infrastructure. He points to Tesla standing up 50,000+ H100/H200 GPUs at Gigafactory Austin as the key catalyst enabling this step-function improvement, applying known LLM scaling techniques to the autonomous driving domain. Baker contends Tesla's proprietary visual training dataset is orders of magnitude larger than Waymo's, creating a durable data moat he compares to owning YouTube plus all of Meta's video properties versus Yahoo — making it structurally difficult for competitors to close the gap. He expresses unusual personal conviction, stating he has never before been willing to make a prediction with this specificity, and expects the outcome to be 'abjectly humiliating' to FSD skeptics, implying TSLA is a strong long given the market is not pricing in this capability inflection.
detail: Baker argues FSD 12.3 (all deep learning, eliminated human code) and 12.5 (runs on AI4 hardware) represent GPT-2 and GPT-2.5 level capability respectively. Tesla is standing up 50,000+ H100/H200s at Gigafactory Austin. With known scaling techniques, Baker expects a rapid jump to GPT-4 equivalent FSD capability. Tesla's proprietary visual training dataset is orders of magnitude larger than Waymo's, creating a durable data moat analogous to owning YouTube plus all of Meta's properties vs. Yahoo.
confidence_evidence: Baker says 'I haven't never been willing to make a prediction like that before' about the 6-18 month timeline, indicating unusual conviction; makes specific version-to-GPT-generation mappings
quote_fragment: abjectly humiliating to everyone who is an FSD skeptic in the next 12 to 18 months

ticker_audit.reasoning: The entire thesis is specifically about Tesla's FSD product, its scaling trajectory, compute buildout, and data moat — TSLA is the direct subject and investment vehicle for this thesis.
